item.title: IGN UK Podcast #283: What Movies You Need to Watch to Prepare for Fallout 4
item.description:
Fallout 4 was announced this week and managed to almost break the internet, but what now? With no firm release date in place, Chris, Rory & Gav offer suggestions for what films you can watch to chase away the post-apocalyptic blues.
Jurassic World director, Colin Trevorrow, also stops by to chat with Krupa briefly about what it was like filling Steven Spielberg's shoes on his new dino action adventure. You can check out the trailer below.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #275: Would Christoph Waltz Lie To Us?!
item.description:
On this week's podcast Rory, Chris & Gav examine the case for and against Christoph Waltz playing Blofeld in Spectre as well as trying not to get too sad about David Lynch leaving the Twin Peaks revival. To be honest, the podcast is worth listening to alone for the bit where Rory sticks on his tinfoil hat to try and decode some cryptic viral marketing.

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #274: Watching Interstellar with Stephen Hawking
item.description:
This week, Chris & Gav discuss the time they watched Interstellar with Prof. Stephen Hawking and whether they now understand Chris Nolan's epic sci-fi movie (*spoilers* they still don't have a clue). Luke gets very upset when he's introduced to a very special robotic guest live on the podcast, and as always the guys pick their favourite things that happened this week in the world of movies, games and just about everything else.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #273: To the Manor Bloodborne
item.description:
Three guys start playing Bloodborne. One's finished Dark Souls, one played a bit, one ran away in fear of From Software. That pretty much sums up this week's podcast crew of Krupa, Gav, and Luke, who have all been spending the week exploring the dark and winding alleyways of Yharnam.

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #272: Becoming Master Criminals
item.description:
Turns out we're excellent at stealth. Seriously. With Battlefield Hardline coming out this week, the podcast crew was invited to a mystery location in Central London to break into a top-secret facility and empty a heavily-guarded vault. We took it far too seriously (apart from Alex who was wearing a bright red hoodie for Comic Relief).
We also talk about how Battlefield's take on Heists compare with those in GTA Online, as well as the massive Nintendo news from earlier in the week and the beautiful PlayStation 4 exclusive Everybody's Gone to the Rapture. As always, that's in addition to the regular nonsense.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #266: Gary Whitta Talks Star Wars & Starlight
item.description:
We really are spoiling you with our pod-guests these days. Hot-on-the-heels of Ex Machina’s Alex Garland and Evolve’s Phil Robb, we’ve now got games journalist-turned-screenwriter Gary Whitta joining Chris and Gav for an epic cast.
Whitta started out in the games industry in the late 1980s before making the transition to film via The Book of Eli and Another Earth. For the last year he’s been busy working in a galaxy far, far away, writing the first standalone movie in the new Star Wars cycle, which will be directed by Gareth ‘Godzilla’ Edwards and hitting screens December 2016.
Gary doesn’t reveal much about the movie – bound as he is by the mother of all non-disclosure agreements – but he does talk about his love of The Empire Strikes Back, how the original trilogy inspired his generation of writers and filmmakers, and what working on this new movie has been like as a creative experience. Oh, and his failure in a Star Wars quiz many moons ago also gets a (somewhat unfair) mention.
If that weren’t enough, Gary offers his advice to aspiring screenwriters, talks about games as an art-form in the 21st Century, explains where’s he’s at with his adaptation of Mark Millar comic Starlight, tells an AMAZING story aboutFrank Darabont, and gives us the skinny on his forthcoming novel Abomination, which is available via pre-sale this Monday, February 9.
Braking Bad spin-off Better Call Saul hits Netflix this week so the boys discuss their hopes and fears for the show, while the conversation about potential TV reboots rumbles on, with Star Trek, The Crystal Maze and Green Wing all getting a mention in reader feedback.
Out this week includes games Gunman Clive 2, Game of Thrones Episode 2 and Evolve, while the big movie releases are Selma, Shaun the Sheep and Jupiter Ascending.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #261: Big Fat Preview of 2015
item.description:
Bloodborne and The Avengers, Evolve and Star Wars, The Witcher 3 and Jurassic World.
Blimey, 2015 is shaping up to be a year of blistering, blockbuster entertainment at home and on the big screen. We thought a methodical appraisal of the year's coming treats was in order, so we printed off a big list, grabbed some highlighters, and sat in room to discuss the lot in chronological order. Sensible fun, we think you'll agree.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #260: Our Favourite Games and Films of 2014
item.description:
If you're anything like us, you may be slightly surprised to find yourself staring down the barrel of 2015. The last few months have been so hectic with big releases and even bigger post-release problems that we haven't really had time to take stock of things.
With this in mind, Luke, Alex and Chris thought it'd be a good time to sit down and run through the biggest and best games and movies that came out this year. It's been a pretty impressive one across the board, yet there are definitely some serious standouts.
So what are you waiting for? Don't forget to join us again next week for the first podcast of 2015, where we'll be running through what's giving us tingles in our nethers for the year ahead.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #259: Jingle Balls
item.description:
Ho ho ho lovely Podpeople! There's a festive feeling in the air as we humans drag our increasingly swollen and hungover carcasses from shop to shop in desperate search of items that will clutter charity shops across the land come January. That's right! It's only bloody Christmas time!
And what Christmas would be complete without your friendly neighborhood Podcast team to help stoke your holiday spirit? So join Luke, Chris and Rory as they discuss everything from the long-awaited news that GTA Online is getting Heists to the continuing saga that is The Interview. We also chat about our favourite Christmas movie moments. One involves pensioner scrotums.
So feel free to relax and download it to listen to at your leisure. This is the last podcast we'll be recording this year, but don't panic! We've got plenty of specials coming up in the next couple of months to tide you over until we return in the New Year.
So from all of us here at IGN UK, thanks for everything this year. Have a fantastic Christmas and we'll see you all in 2015!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #252: The Podcasting Hour
item.description:
To celebrate Halloween, we've recorded a horror special, but before we get to the scary stuff, Chris, Dan and new boy Gav discuss all things Marvel. With the studio announcing their Phase 3 slate - which includes standalone movies for Doctor Strange, Captain Marvel and Black Panther - there's lots to talk about on the superhero front.
And as the podcast is dropping on October 31, we discuss our perfect Halloween night in, which involves cheese, 1980s horror, and a young Krupa dressing up as a ghost and standing on his own in the garden. Sad times.
Reader feedback covers IGN's review process (again) movie and game soundtracks (again) and some new stuff that we won't spoil here.
As for the week's new releases, we've for Sunset Overdrive, Walking Dead, NBA Live and more on the game front, while Ouja, Horns and the brilliant Nightcrawler his cinema screens...
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #245: It's All in The Wrist
item.description:
Hello chums! It's Friday, it's autumn, it's been a busy week, so what better way to cope with everything that's going on by listening to us lot desperately try to wrap our heads round it all.
Yes, Alex, Krupa, Luke and Chris return once more to cover this week's biggest news, from the PS4 passing a milestone in our green and pleasant land, all the way to the Arkham Knight release date being somewhat later than originally planned. We've diversified this week into covering niche interests, which is why we're also chatting about Apple's little event and this small indie game called Destiny. Come and be educated!
All of this is, as usual, topped off with a bumper load of your excellent feedback. So sit back, kick up your heels, and prepare for the most intense aurgasm (it's a mix of aural and orgasm) of your life. Or something. Enjoy!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.description:
Welcome, intrepid traveller.
You may have noticed there was no podcast last week. That definitely wasn't down to a technical error or anything like that. This week the podcast has been handled with extra care; it's been uploaded in both video and audio form for your sensory pleasure.
So join Chris, Krupa and Rich as they round-up the biggest news, pick their favourite genre movie mash-ups and take a look at the week's big releases.

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #242: Hands-Off My 'Nuts
item.description:
Hello friends! It's Friday, which can only mean one thing - the IGN UK Podcast is back to assault your senses in the most delightful way.
This week it's Luke, Krupa, Alex and Chris in the hot seats, dissecting all the biggest stories of the week. Pressing matters include the leaking of Assassin's Creed Rogue and the news about Resident Evil getting remastered, as well as the speculation about Studio Ghibli's future. It's a thrilling time.
There's also a fair bit of discussion about doughnuts and what Alex got up to on his holibobs. For those of you watching the video version, you'll note he's looks like he's been varnished a shade of rich mahogany. Do not adjust your sets, that's actually how he looks. We're as confused as you.
So sit back, relax, and let us built an auditory life raft upon which you can float atop all your worldly woes.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #240: A-Holes Assemble
item.description:
It's Friday, which means the IGN UK Podcast team have once again roused themselves from a collective stupor to shower you with bants, reflection and insight. Mainly the first one, if we're honest.
This week sees Luke, Daniel, Chris and Rich get the trusty mics out in order to discuss everything from the rumoured Star Wars: Episode VII plot, to the official Doom 4 details, all via Marvel's proposed release schedule. We also have a good old chinwag about whether Comic-Con has evolved so much that it's no longer fulfilling its original purpose as a fan festival.
We tie it all up by advising you what games and films you should be looking out for, and answering your feedback. Although at this point I'd like to make it abundantly clear that I can grow facial hair. Lots of lovely, long facial hair. I'm like Gandalf in that respect. The fact I'm magical and also possess my own staff is a happy coincidence, however.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #237: I(GN), Robot
item.description:
You came back. You really came back for another week?
Don't get me wrong, we're happy you did, and your blind loyalty is rewarded by the introduction of the latest member of the team: IGN Robot, who reviews this week's game and film releases.
But there's more. We condense the week's news into a information rich 10 minutes, before tackling the subject of summer blockbusters and what their future looks like. Transformers: Age of Extinction is a huge commercial success but a critical flop. So are blockbusters 100% critic proof? Is there still room for smart summer entertainment, like the forthcoming Dawn of the Planet of the Apes? We discuss how the summer popcorn movie has changed and will continue to grow.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #236: British Laughs and Global Leaks
item.description:
Hello again you lucky things. Yes, it's that time of week again, where Luke, Alex, Krupa and Rich gather to talk all things entertainment-related for your audible enjoyment.
This week's been an interesting one, with rumours and leaks cropping up all over the place. So many leaks, in fact, we've taken to wearing galoshes to work in a bid to stop the water from damaging our lovely suede shoes. I write this from a paddling pool raft, adrift in an ocean of speculation.
Top of the list for dissection are the mutterings about the new Star Wars: Episode VII villains, the big Witcher 3 file leaks and reports that Matt Damon is set to return to the Bourne franchise. We also discuss the new Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles trailer, obviously, which segues rather nicely into a chat about our famous British comedies of all time. If you're at a bit of a low ebb this weekend, there's definitely some recommendations here that'll brighten your day.
So what are you waiting for?! Dive on in. Hopefully there won't be any audio issues this week but, if there are, it's Rich's fault. So send us advice on how he should be punished. Until next week!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #235: Games, Goals and Gouging
item.description:
Happy Friday, friends! Yes, it's that time of week again and we've got a brand new episode of the IGN UK Podcast ready to ease its into your lugholes. Exciting times ahead.
This week Luke, Krupa, Alex and Chris discuss a number of things, from how Luke and Krupa coped with their first E3 to how Tilly is preparing for his imminent trip to Brazil to watch England's third match. Given last night's performance he's probably slightly less excited now.
We've also got a bumper amount of feedback from you guys, with you telling us your favourite moments from our podcasting history and what you made of E3. It also leads to a revelation from Luke that he only plays Mortal Kombat for the story. This does not go down well.
We hope you enjoy it and have fantastic weekends! See you same time, same place, next week.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #233: The Generation Game
item.description:
It's only that time of week again, you lucky young things! Yes, it's Friday and the bountiful IGN UK Podcast has once again been tenderly prepared to soothingly flow into your ears like lovely milk of magnesia on nasty, scratchy skin.
Oh, the treats we have in store for you. Luke, Alex and Krupa are here to discuss all the week's delays and leaks, and we even find time to discuss the fact that Edgar Wright has left Ant-Man. I know. I thought their love was eternal too.
The talking point this week is all about our personal favourite games of the last generation and we also have a bumper load of your feedback to get through, along with the round-up of this week's releases.
All of this wrapped up in the finest good humour and tied tight with a bow of hysteria. So what are you waiting for? Get on it and we'll see you next week!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices
item.content:encoded:
It's only that time of week again, you lucky young things! Yes, it's Friday and the bountiful IGN UK Podcast has once again been tenderly prepared to soothingly flow into your ears like lovely milk of magnesia on nasty, scratchy skin.

Oh, the treats we have in store for you. Luke, Alex and Krupa are here to discuss all the week's delays and leaks, and we even find time to discuss the fact that Edgar Wright has left Ant-Man. I know. I thought their love was eternal too.

The talking point this week is all about our personal favourite games of the last generation and we also have a bumper load of your feedback to get through, along with the round-up of this week's releases.

All of this wrapped up in the finest good humour and tied tight with a bow of hysteria. So what are you waiting for? Get on it and we'll see you next week!

Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #231: Wolfenstein Special
item.description:
Pack it up, pack it in, and let me begin. This week’s podcast is a Wolfenstein: New Order special, while the show itself will look and sound a little like this...
NEWS
- Pentagon Planning for Zombie Attack!
- Star Wars Image Released
- Far Cry 4 Box Art Controversy
- Hugh Jackman's Avengers Dream
- Halo 5 Guardians Update
READER FEEDBACK
We tackle the big subjects - namely Craft Beer, Robocop and Visionaries. Again.
WHAT YOU SHOULD BE WATCHING/PLAYING
X-Men: Days of Future Past, Transistor, and the work of Adam Sandler are out this week and discussed at length.
As always we'd love to hear from you on ign_ukfeedback@ign.com, by posting your feedback in the comments below or by contacting us on Facebook or Twitter. Thanks, and Duck Hunt!

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #229: Staff Wars - A Lost Hope
item.description:
You've all seen some of that picture - the biggest names in Hollywood, sat around on comfy chairs, deep in discussion about one of the most anticipated releases in entertainment history. But enough of this week's IGN UK podcast and it's hosts Alex, Stu, Krupa and Chris, spare a thought for the new and old (and very old) faces of Star Wars Episode 7, which is this week's hot topic of conversation. Will it live up to the hype, or should we approach with excited caution, much like we are the Justice League movie?
Elsewhere, there's a bucket-load of reader feedback in which we continue our search for the UK's oldest gamer (surprise! It's not Stu), plus we chew over the week's news.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #228: Stream of the Crop
item.description:
On this week’s podcast Stu, Luke and Chris discuss how we consume our movies, games and TV, with Sky, Amazon Prime, iTunes, the iPlayer and Netflix all coming under consideration.
There's a wealth of other good stuff too. We chat about the Flash Gordon reboot, whether we need to talk about game resolutions anymore, and run through your most shocking TV moments and favourite finales (all appropriately headed up with SPOILER warnings).
Apologies in advance for Luke's apparent incompetence, he's still in holiday mode, though it does result in a cracking Oscar Pistorius reference.
So what are you waiting for? Download the audio to listen at your leisure. Hope you've all had fantastic Easter breaks, and we'll see you next week!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #222 with Terry Gilliam
item.description:
This week on the IGN UK Podcast we're join by the brilliant British director (yes, we've fully claimed him) Terry Gilliam, who joins Stu to talk about his latest film, Zero Theorem – a dystopian view of the supposedly utopian future.
Meanwhile, Krupa and Luke take a look at the winners and losers from this week's Games BAFTA awards and all the news from the events.

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #221: Adele Dazeem is a Single Lady?!
item.description:
This week's Podcast features just Stu and Luke as everyone else has abandoned us. I should warn you now, the hysteria hits early and just gets worse.
It's either the greatest thing we've ever created or the absolute worst.
Topics up for discussion range from the announcement of Arkham Knight, The Oscar winners and losers, and also why we seem to be inundated with next-gen remakes. It also goes slightly off-piste around the time that Stuart reveals Adele Dazeem is in the throes of divorce and Luke pretty much loses his sh**. It's still unclear at this time if he will ever fully recover.
Also Stuart put all the cables in right for once, so we have a video version once more! We even did some visual trickery on you, getting Luke to sit closer to the camera so he looks normal sized and not like a Dwarf. We learned that one by watching the extras on The Fellowship of the Ring. Also if you're looking for the picture Luke mentions about Travolta and Cumberbatch it's here. So watch or listen you lucky things, and we'll see you next week!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #219: Lionheads or Tales?
item.description:
Happy Friday friends and readers! It's that time of week again, when IGN UK's finest descend upon our microphones to bring you whimsy and insanity in equal measure. This week, we've even managed to coerce a couple of Lionhead's finest into joining us!
We start off with Luke, Stu and Chris dissecting the week's biggest news, from the shock revelation that BioShock creator Irrational is hugely downsizing to the Titanfall announcements, and we even find time to chat about the Fantastic Four casting.
Following that, Alex and Krupa sit down with the esteemed Tim and Craig from Fable Anniversary developer Lionhead to chat all about the game and life at the studio.
Then it's back to LSC (see, it's like a drug but not 'cos we're good boys) to guide you home. Oh what larks await you! There may not be a full video version this week for reasons beyond our control (or continuing incompetence on our part) but you can check out the segment with the Fable boys below, then click through the link or download to listen at your leisure!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #217: The Reservoir Dogs Show
item.description:
Let us start this week with an apology. Two apologies in fact. First up, there's no video for reasons we won't go into. It certainly wasn't down to incompetence though, nor was it because Stuart is insecure he hasn't had a haircut in about four months. Nope. Definitely neither of those.
The second apology is that the sound is a bit dodgy this week. Which is a shame as there's a fair amount of hilarity as Stu, Chris and Luke cover all the news from the Lex Luthor rumours to the lack of multiplayer in games like The Order: 1886. We also chat about the misogyny that is The Expendabelles and the decision to redesign Sonic and the gang. For the love of God, someone show Knuckles some leg exercises.
There's also a lot of chat about Reservoir Dogs, as Chris and Stu apparently don't know any other films.
Normal sound quality and video will return next week, thanks for bearing with us! In the meantime, download or click below to listen at your leisure. Enjoy your weekends, and we'll see you next week!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #216: Slim Pickings
item.description:
There's been a lot happening this week. A lorra lorra lot. So much, in fact, that we rushed to get through it all and accidentally managed to make the podcast shorter than usual. Whoops. Still. Think of it as a more concentrated offering, like Splenda. Splenda for your EARS. Yum.
So, in this Splend-iferous episode of the podcast, Luke, Alex and Chris tackle everything that's been going on this week from Nintendo's ongoing financial struggles to all Microsoft's Xbox One rumours, from the exciting announcement of the PS Vita Slim (worst kept secret, ever) to the... interesting X-Men images that came out this week. Are we sure the dude playing Toad doesn't have mumps? I mean, has anyone actually checked he's okay?
So have a listen, or a watch if that's more your thing, as we ramble our way along and as usual narrowly avoid ballsing it all up. On the plus side, no one nearly sh**s themselves this week. Which is always a plus.
Enjoy!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #215: Toilet Humour
item.description:
It's that time of week again! Yes, the IGN UK Podcast has once more arrived to burrow into your earholes like a curious cockroach.
This week sees Luke, Stu, Alex and Chris in the hot seats, taking on your feedback and discussing the biggest game and movie stories of the day. What are our thoughts about Mario not coming to smartphones? Have we hidden all the sharp objects following the delay of Batman vs. Superman? We know the answers, but we're not telling you. You'll just have to listen to find out!
There's also an unfortunate moment where one of our trusty crew is laid low by the call of nature. Try not to judge him too harshly, for it can happen to the best of us.
Enjoy!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.description:
Welcome to the podcast – it's lovely to see you, intrepid listener (or viewer). This week Krupa, Alex, and Stu take the pod by the proverbial nuts and take down a monster bag of feedback and tackle the most pressing issues of the day. Not really, it's mainly video game and that sort of thing.
So don't delay. The podcast has been released!

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #212: Sherlock Who?
item.description:
A new year and a new episode of the IGN UK Podcast – yes, really all your dreams have suddently come true.
With most people still on their holidays, strung out on novelty craft ales and distended by obscene portions of re-heated turkey, it's up to Stu, Luke, and Krupa to look back and take stock of the Christmas break.
Festive TV is put under the spotlight, particularly Doctor Who and Sherlock, and we go through all of your feedback on what Nintendo needs to do to salvage the Wii U, if that's still even a possibility.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast Special: Big Fat Quiz Of The Year
item.description:
It's Christmas time and that means it's a time for peace, reflection and soul-searching. And what better way to do all those things than in a bloody novelty quiz?!
Yes, for the second year running us IGN UK folk decided to sunder ourselves into two teams and pit our wits against each other in an intense battle for dominance. Will Alex and Chris emerge victorious? Or are Krupa and Luke destined for glory? Will Stu make it through hosting duties without a drink?! Who can say, but it does get a bit Road to Damascus when the anagram round crops up.
As it's Christmas, we've decided to offer you this podcast in video form, which is the way the baby Jesus would have wanted you to enjoy it. If you're stuck in your ways though, then never fear! The standard audio link is also present.
So have a listen, play along and make sure you have an absolutely cracking Christmas. We'll be back in your lug-holes in 2014 so until then take care of yourselves, wrap up warm and keep smiling.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #211: Keza Mac-Gone-Ald
item.description:
Friends, a sad day is upon us. Yes, today marks the last time you'll ever hear the lovely Keza MacDonald on the IGN UK Podcast. She leaves us now, but will continue to write for the site in future. Which is nice. I wish I could say it's to a much better place she goes, but sadly it's Brighton. Can't win 'em all, K-dawg.
Still, before we get too morose (after all, Christmas is just around the corner), be reassured that there's a bumper crop of joy on this week's Podcast, as Luke, Alex and Stu relive Keza's best memories, discuss our favourite games from 2013 and talk about how to commemorate Keza. My idea of a Viking funeral involving her desk in the Thames doesn't go down well.
As an added bonus, we're all hungover! That's right! It's going to be one of those Podcasts! So don't deny yourselves the exquisite aural pleasure we've cultivated for you any longer. Click below to get listening, and head back to IGN on Christmas Eve to check out our Christmas Quiz! It proves beyond any reasonable doubt that we have been paying absolutely no attention to what's been going on in the last 12 months. Here's to a similar 2014!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #210: Dropping Like Flies
item.description:
It's a bumper edition of the podcast this week, where Alex, Chris, Keza and Luke discuss everything from Microsoft's Xbox One sales to the... interesting VGX awards ceremony.
We also reveal the sad news that the wonderful Keza MacDonald will be leaving us full time, becoming a contributing editor in the New Year. You'll still see her stuff around the site, it just means she won't be podcasting with us no more.
But don't worry! There are happy things too! There's interviews with the Trade-In Detectives, a team working to get you the best value second hand games, and Richard Armitage AKA Mr Thorin Oakenshield from The Hobbit: Desolation of Smaug.
We even discuss whether we're Directioners or Beliebers. It's a very exciting time.
So what are you waiting for you lucky things? Click below to get listening!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

We even discuss whether we're Directioners or Beliebers. It's a very exciting time.
So what are you waiting for you lucky things? Click below to get listening!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices
item.content:encoded:
It's a bumper edition of the podcast this week, where Alex, Chris, Keza and Luke discuss everything from Microsoft's Xbox One sales to the... interesting VGX awards ceremony.

We also reveal the sad news that the wonderful Keza MacDonald will be leaving us full time, becoming a contributing editor in the New Year. You'll still see her stuff around the site, it just means she won't be podcasting with us no more.

But don't worry! There are happy things too! There's interviews with the Trade-In Detectives, a team working to get you the best value second hand games, and Richard Armitage AKA Mr Thorin Oakenshield from The Hobbit: Desolation of Smaug.

We even discuss whether we're Directioners or Beliebers. It's a very exciting time.

So what are you waiting for you lucky things? Click below to get listening!

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #209: Frozen Potter
item.description:
On this week's IGN UK Podcast we've got two special interviews. The first is with the directors of the really wonderful Frozen – Chris Buck and Jennifer Lee, who also wrote Wreck-It Ralph – and Daniel Radcliffe and Dane DeHaan, the stars of Kill Your Darlings.
And in addition to those big interviews, we've got all the usual news and feedback for your enjoyment.

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #207: Next-Gen Is Here
item.description:
Welcome to the IGN UK podcast on this most auspicious of days: the launch of the Xbox One.
In this week's show we talk about Microsoft's new console in great detail and relay our first experiences with the box and the games.
But there's also time to talk about some of the week's big news: that Uncharted, the new RoboCop trailer, the strong early sales of the PS4 and, of course, the anniversary of our beloved Doctor Who.
IGN UK Podcast #206
(right click to save)
Subscribe to IGN's Podcast Series

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #206: Bringing You PlayStation MORE, Xbox NONE
item.description:
This week we compare BioWare’s PR tactics to prolonged ejaculation, useless tat that comes with collector’s editions and the fact that some big stuff’s apparently about to happen? We wish someone had told us.
We also delve into the world of entertainment by talking about concept art for Batman Versus Superman. There are pictures too, which you can’t see, but we gesticulated at them pretty hard during recording and that probably comes across.
So stop being lazy and expecting us to do the hard work for you. Sit yourself down and let Stu, Luke, Alex and Keza whisk you away on a magical sensory journey that’ll put hair on your chest and tickle your bifkin. Or whatever. It should raise a grin, at least.
IGN UK Podcast #206
(right click to save)
Subscribe to IGN's Podcast Series
As always we'd love to hear from you on ign_ukfeedback@ign.com, by posting your feedback in the comments below or by contacting us on Facebook or Twitter. Thanks!

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #205: Brought To You In 1080p
item.description:
On this week's eagerly awaited podcast we look back at the life and times of one Thomas Butler. He's not dead, just dead to us as he moves on to pastures new.
We chat about the differences between 720p and 1080p and its effect on your gaming experience, plus we have a chat with celebrated crime author Ian Rankin on the release of new novella Kickback City.
So don't just sit there gawping at the screen, let Stu, Luke, Krupa and Tom take you on a journey of unimaginable pleasure. Or something.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #204: Happy Plastice'en
item.description:
Plucked eyeballs. Disembowelment. Vampire lavatories. Such things are everyday occurrences for our special guest Lee Hardcastle, a man responsible for more violent Plasticine deaths than Aardman's local recycling centre. He's here to chat to Stu, Chris and Luke about short film Ghost Burger and the disproportionate advantage of banana skins in Mario Kart.
Also this week, Tom speaks to Thor: The Dark World director Alan Taylor and producer Kevin Feige about the Marvel Universe and there's a bonus selection of IGN staff's spookiest happenings.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #203: Ender's Game Special
item.description:
In this special edition of the podcast, we sit down with director Gavin Hood and producer Roberto Orci to discuss their new film Ender’s Game.
Starring Harrison Ford, Asa Butterfield, Ben Kingsley, Viola Davis, Hailee Steinfield and Abigail Breslin, the film is based on Orson Scott Card’s bestselling science-fiction novel about a gifted 12-year-old who is trained to become earth’s ultimate military leader in the fight against an alien invasion.
In this extended interview we discuss the difficulties of adapting such a beloved book and talk about working with big-screen legends Kinsley and Ford.
We also discuss Orci’s forthcoming projects The Amazing Spider-Man 2 and reboots of Van Helsing and The Mummy, while Hood discusses the challenges he faced on the set of X-Men Origins: Wolverine.
So enjoy this sci-fi themed edition of the pod, and rest assured it will be business-as-usual next week.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #202
item.description:
In this week’s stripped-down podcast, Stu and Chris discuss the end of Breaking Bad, debating whether or not the show had a fitting climax. We’ve also got an interview with Alan Moore, who discusses new book Fashion Beauty and the way in which Hollywood has treated his material.
On top of that the guys also discuss...
- Guillermo Del Toro's Simpsons- The passing of Tom Clancy- GTA's online issues- 24 shooting in London
And reader feedback includes talk of Zack Snyder, Gamesmaster and Speedball 2. So what are you waiting for? Get downloading and get listening!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #201: True Blooded
item.description:
The gods were smiling upon the IGN UK podcast this week as we were blessed with not one, but TWO special guests: IGN Twitter wizard Eriq Martin, visiting from San Francisco, and Rob Kazinsky, fresh from the set of True Blood - though you might also know him as Chuck Hansen from Pacific Rim or, yes, Sean Slater from Eastenders. Join them and boring, normal old Alex, Keza and Tom as they discuss a multitude of nonsense, including:
Rob's frankly quite debilitating addiction to video games
GTA V and GTA Online
Steambox
The surprisingly awesome trailer for the Need for Speed movie
Treasured pieces of games merchandise
Eastenders (but only a bit)
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast: FU Special
item.description:
We'll level with you. We've neglected the mailbag for too long, and it seems loads more people have started writing of late. So we thought we'd take the opportunity to rummage around in the big old sack and answer some of the most pressing questions.
And we of course tackle the issue of the Nintendo 2DS. So join Alex, Krupa, and Tom for some informative banter.

item.content:encoded:
We'll level with you. We've neglected the mailbag for too long, and it seems loads more people have started writing of late. So we thought we'd take the opportunity to rummage around in the big old sack and answer some of the most pressing questions.

And we of course tackle the issue of the Nintendo 2DS. So join Alex, Krupa, and Tom for some informative banter.


IGN UK Podcast: FU Special – 69.9 MB

item.title: IGN UK Podcast Gamescom 2013 Special
item.description:
Welcome to a very special instalment of the IGN UK Podcast live from Gamescom (well, it was live when we recorded it). We’ve braved the hordes of ravenous gamers, savouring the very best of what the show has to offer, and in the process Alex has become more than a little obsessed with Titanfall. But that’s not all, Keza’s played with some Pokémon, Krupa’s beaten Alex at FIFA, and Rich… well, Rich has just eaten a lot of German food and done a lot of editing.
IGN UK Podcast Gamescom Special 2013 – 63.9 MB

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #198: The Truth Is Out There
item.description:
Daniel, Stu, and Tom are joined by special guest Nick Pope this week. Formerly the British government's UFO expert, Nick is in the studio to talk about the science fact behind The Bureau: XCOM Declassified, the possibility of alien invasion, and all things extra-terrestrial.
Other topics up for discussion in this week's episode of the UK's biggest and best entertainment podcast include:
Grand Theft Auto Online
Call of Duty: Ghosts multiplayer
Xbox One's Kinect requirements
Lex Luthor (again)
There's also the usual tangents and asides, including details of the Lincoln Ritz's pre-film adverts circa 1990 (Shakespeare Street Garage, Shakespeare Street, Lincoln). Listen now.
IGN UK Podcast 198: The Truth Is Out There
(right click to save)

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #197: Critic Proof
item.description:
Join Daniel, Stu, and Chris for the very first podcast recorded in our brand-new studio.
We discuss the exciting announcement that Peter Capaldi will be the twelfth Doctor, who we think would make a brilliant Lex Luthor, and why Microsoft needed to include a headset with the Xbox One.
There's also the usual tangents and asides, including more clarity on the issue of posting meat. Listen now.

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #196: Now With 100% More Badgers
item.description:
Welcome fearless traveller. You're about to embark on a journey into the dark, into the unknown. Actually, this week has some semblance of order as we talk about the big news of the week:
Nintendo's dire quarterly financials
BioShock DLC revealed
Fez II unexpectedly cancelled
And if that wasn't enough to be getting on with, we also talk about the future of Nintendo in general, go through loads of Reader FU, and of course, we've give you an insight into the new office if you're interested (you're probably not, but be polited and say 'yes').
It's all here for the taking in this week's podcast, so join Alex, Krupa, Keza, and Stu... they've been expecting you.
IGN UK Podcast #196: Now With 100% More Badgers – 67.4 MB
(right click to save)

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #195: Comic-Con Round-Up
item.description:
Forget what we said last week - this is our last podcast in the old office. And it's a belter, featuring Stu, Dan and Chris talking about all the major announcements at Comic-Con, most notably the Batman v Superman movie that will be hitting our screens in 2015.
If that weren't enough, the team also talks:
Guardians of the Galaxy
Putting a Shrek in it
The Avengers 2
World of Warcraft
Catching Fire
Our Desert Island Games (kind of)
Breaking Bad
The IGN comments section
And more! Download right now for your listening pleasure, and join us next week from what we promise will be a brand new location. with soundproofing!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #194: The End of an Era
item.description:
Say goodbye to the trademark sirens in the background of your favourite podcast: this is the last ever podcast we'll do from our current London office, home of IGN UK for the past five years. From next week we'll be somewhere brand new with actual equipment that works and SOUNDPROOFING. It's the end of an era.
Join Stu, Luke and Keza and celebrate the end of an era with chat about:
-THE HEAT, OH GOD THE HEAT
-Kojima's Twitter tears
-Grand Theft Auto, again
-Older games that aren't terrible
-Luke's terrible taste in music
Hashpigs: The bacon that gets you stoned!
Download right now for your listening pleasure, and join us next week from a brand new location. Which we'll definitely have set up in time.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #193: Somerset Shandy
item.description:
It's a mini-edition of the IGN UK Podcast this week, but it's still packed full of good stuff, like a yoghurt. Luke, Keza and Tom are in Animal Crossing's thrall, whilst Rich is busy impressing celebrities with his card tricks (not a euphemism).
This week we discuss:
-Glastonbury and the invention of the "Somerset Shandy"
-Dom Mattrick's Xbox exit
-Lovefilm's unwise abandonment of game rentals
Download and listen right here, and come back next week for more, when we'll be back at full strength. Until then!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #192: Full of Burgers
item.description:
It's been a long week... and we haven't recorded a podcast on a Friday afternoon for a long time, so forgive us. Please forgive us.
But fear not, Alex has returned and tell us all about E3 from the front lines. We also talk about the week in news, Seth Rogen dropping in to talk about This Is The End and The Last of Us, and George Osbourne bizarrely.

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #191: Xbox 180
item.description:
The big news of the week (and, indeed, they year) dominates the latest IGN UK podcast: Microsoft's extraordinary reversal on basically every unpopular new aspect of the Xbox One. But we're not one-trick ponies here. We also chat about:
-Scary robot sex toys-70s sci-fi-Animal Crossing-Mary Jane, the Spider-Man character, no the other thing)
Be sure to come back next week for more. Until then!

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #190: E 2 Brute
item.description:
Been following all the big announcements at E3 from your bedroom? Yeah, us too – so while Keza and Alex have been busy in the LA sun, we've been stuck in London. But it's not all bad, Daniel, Tom, and Rich headed down to London's finest video game hangout, the Loading bar in Soho for a special live podcast.
It's a little bit noisy, but you can make out most of what we're rambling on about. We talk about Etoo – a special event that took place for those us stuck back in Blighty – and we dissect each of the main press conferences, picking our highs and lows of what's been a very eventful week.
So get your sound holes around this:
IGN UK Podcast #190: E 2 Brute – 82.9 MB
(right click to save)
Subscribe to IGN's Podcast Series
Also, our email address for podcast feedback (ignukfeedback@ign.com) is temporarily down. We're getting it sorted – promise. Until then, please leave feedback in the comments below or contact us on Facebook or Twitter. Thanks!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

Been following all the big announcements at E3 from your bedroom? Yeah, us too – so while Keza and Alex have been busy in the LA sun, we've been stuck in London. But it's not all bad, Daniel, Tom, and Rich headed down to London's finest video game hangout, the Loading bar in Soho for a special live podcast.

It's a little bit noisy, but you can make out most of what we're rambling on about. We talk about Etoo – a special event that took place for those us stuck back in Blighty – and we dissect each of the main press conferences, picking our highs and lows of what's been a very eventful week.

So get your sound holes around this:

IGN UK Podcast #190: E 2 Brute – 82.9 MB

(right click to save)

Subscribe to IGN's Podcast Series 

Also, our email address for podcast feedback (ignukfeedback@ign.com) is temporarily down. We're getting it sorted – promise. Until then, please leave feedback in the comments below or contact us on Facebook or Twitter. Thanks!

Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #189: E3 For All
item.description:
So not much is really happening next week. Some games are being announced or summat. Now, we jest, it's E3 – the week the internet melts.
In this week's podcast we look forward to each and every press conference, and make some pretty bold predications which will inevitably be proved hideously wrong. So join Stu, Tom, and Krupa for a round-up of what will be going down next week. What games does Xbox One have up its sleeve? PS4 – what will it look like? Can Nintendo win everyone around? And what's Apple up to at WWDC?
And on top of that, it would be remiss of us not to talk about Games of Thrones and THAT episode. Don't worry, though, all spoilers come right at the end of the podcast and we give you sufficient warning. We're not horrible people and we don't want to spoil it for you.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #188: By the Rivers of Babble On
item.description:
Hello, you lovely, beautiful people, and welcome to the IGNUK podcast.
This week our numbers have been depleted but don't worry the quality hasn't suffered one iota – that remains impeccably shoddy.
So join Stu, Keza, and Krupa as they discuss everything from next-gen launch prices to Bid TV, Arrested Development to the philosophy of internet memes. There's even time to talk about Peter Molyneux's lifechanging box.
That's all on this week's podcast, so do yourself a favour and download it now. Go on, it's Friday and you deserve a treat:
IGN UK Podcast #188: By the Rivers of Babble On – 45.4 MB

item.title: IGN UK Podcast 186: It's Coming...
item.description:
With less than a week to go before we finally get concrete details on what the next Xbox will be, Alex, Tom and Stu take one last swing at what they want the console to do. One thing's for sure, it won't play Gran Turismo 6, which was announced this week, but you'd hope it's future is slightly brighter than the Wii U's, which took another knock when EA announced it had zero Wii U games in development. None. Nada.
But hey, don't feel sad! Put a smile on your face by sitting back, popping on your headphones and giving this week's IGN UK podcast a listen...
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #185: The Director's Cut
item.description:
Xbox might be called Infinity. The World's coming to an end. And Thor might be reluctant to get back into the gym and make extremely bland omelettes.
In light of seeing the brilliantly barmy Richard Donner cut of Superman II, we also get round to talking all about our favourite Director's Cuts.
Intrigued? You should be.
Daniel, Chris, Stu and Keza also round-up the week's hottest news and cast their eyes over the upcoming games and movies, so you know what to spend your hard-earned on this weekend.
So what are you waiting for? Treat those gorgeous ears right now.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #184: GTAgasm
item.description:
Welcome, friends. Settle down, put on some headphones, and listen to the IGN UK team talk about the day a few weeks ago when we saw Grand Theft Auto 5. We've been bottling up our feelings, but now it's time to let it all out. Daniel can finally express his long-held wish for a game that combines good scuba-diving and Hollywood heist moments. Alex can froth over falling out of planes and wrestling bears. Keza can bibble about how good the countryside looks. And we can all share our conviction that Trevor might be the best thing ever. Alex, Daniel, Keza and a very hungover Chris also chat about EVE Fanfest, a potential new Inbetweeners movie, piracy and Nintendo's E3 no-show, amongst the usual nonsense.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #182: In A Podcast Far, Far Away
item.description:
This week on the IGN UK podcast we talk about all the announcements Nintendo made for the 3DS – new Zelda and a new Yoshi's Island – and why it's quickly becoming one of our favourite platforms.
But that's not all, we go through the big new features for FIFA 14 (sadly, it's not set in the 80s) and the prospect of having a new Star Wars movie every summer. Too much?
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #181: Spontaneously Combusting in Your Ears
item.description:
This week the IGN UK crew endeavour to sort the wheat from the chafe in terms of Xbox 720 rumours. Will it always be on? Can it detect your eyes? Will it be backwards compatible? To be honest, your guess is as good as ours, but we sure had fun speculating about it.
Alex, Chris, Rich and Stu also examine two of 2013’s biggest sci-fi flicks in the shape of Oblivion and Elysium, talk Dark Souls 2 and Far Cry 3, and find out just what is missing from Zack Snyder’s forthcoming superhero reboot Man of Steel.
And if that weren’t enough, we also review the latest film and game releases and discuss the dangers of spontaneous combustion. So get downloading and listening to the best IGN UK podcast you’ll hear this week.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #179: It's Snaaake, You Muppets!
item.description:
This week has been a beauty if, like most of the IGN UK crew, you have eyes that work. Trailers for Battlefield 4 and Metal Gear Solid V: The Phantom Pain have both dropped like a fresh seagull dropping from San Francisco, causing this week's podcast team of Alex, Daniel, Chris and Rich to go 'ooh' and 'aah' a lot.
Also in the chatter-cannon firing line is this week's news including a really detailed breakdown on the new GTA V screens, PS Vita's indie resurrection and Chris' visit to the set of the new Muppet movie featured David Brent himself, Ricky Gervais.
So what are you waiting for? Treat your ears now.

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #178: Tom Braider Returns
item.description:
It's been out for a few weeks, and we've all played it so let's talk Tomb Raider. It's fair to say we all liked it, but some way more than others. For a full, frank, spoilerific discussion download the podcast now or wait for it to pop up on iTunes.
But if you've not played Lara's latest adventure, don't worry, we stil chat about the week's news from free EA games atoning for the SimCity debacle to Deus Ex heading to Wii U.
So what are you waiting for? Tickle your ears now. And make sure if you do nothing else, you listen to the very end of the podcast to hear Alex's God of War video review outtakes.

But if you've not played Lara's latest adventure, don't worry, we stil chat about the week's news from free EA games atoning for the SimCity debacle to Deus Ex heading to Wii U. So what are you waiting for? Tickle your ears now. And make sure if you do nothing else, you listen to the very end of the podcast to hear Alex's God of War video review outtakes.

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #177: Monsters, Gods and Cities
item.description:
It’s IGN UK Podcast time again and our thrillsome topic this week is… urban planning! Or rather, the lack of planning that seems to have resulted in SimCity’s woeful launch, which has been a full-on fiasco.
We also chat about why the Bioshock movie didn’t happen, and whether it should have, Nintendo’s apparent patent infringement, the Marvel Heroes Arena Spectacular Stage Show and the perils of portraying Spiderman, Keza and Luke’s experiences monster hunting, and why Alex never wants to look at God of War ever again.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #176 With Jonathan Ross
item.description:
Jonathan Ross joins us for the usual melange of games, comics and movies chat.
Remember when we said we were going to have a special guest on the podcast a few weeks ago? It’s happened – we got TV’s motor-mouthed and geeky-brained Jonathan Ross in to talk to us about his new games and comics projects and, of course, them video games.
We even filmed it for your added enjoyment. Take a look at our wee faces:
We chat about the mobile games studio that Jonathan’s been involved with and its recently re-released game, Catcha Catcha Aliens, Miyazaki and Japanese films, celebrity name-dropping, Assassin’s Creed 4: Black Flag, microtransactions, superhero films, Tomb Raider, a bit of Bioshock and the coming year’s big movies and games. Seriously, we really went on a bit. You can listen or download below.
We’ll be back next week sans Ross - but there's plenty to digest in the meantime. Send us your feeback on Facebook and Twitter, and email us at ignukfeedback@ign.com.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #174: PS4 is Here (Sort of)
item.description:
Welcome to the IGN UK Podcast. This week we're joined by an old friend of the podcast, Mr. Olly Moss. And it's just as well, as there was quite a bit to talk about this week – you know – with the PlayStation 4 being announced and all.
So join Krupa, Luke, Tom, and Olly as we breakdown the press conference and pore over all those tantalising announcements.

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #173: User-Generated Content
item.description:
We're not entirely sure what happened this week, but although you guys are usually pretty good at speaking your minds, this week it was completely out of control. We got tonnes of feedback. All at once. In droves. It was brilliant.
So mosey on down and listen to a podcast spectacular in which we answer all your questions. We also speculate about what we'll see next week at the possible/probable PS4 announcement event, talk about the new games in the Donkey Kong and Batman: Arkham series that were unveiled this week, and mull over whether there's anyone left at this stage who hasn't been cast in X-Men: Days of Future Past.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #172: The Podcast Strikes Back
item.description:
Welcome to another IGN UK Podcast.
Today, we're talking about Rayman Legends going multiplatform (what does this mean for the Wii U?), the mooted price of the PlayStation 4, the potential for a Boba Fett and Yoda spinoff movie, and the prospect of a new Assassin's Creed game set in a new historical period. Does anyone else fancy an AC game set in the 1970s with platform boots and DISCO beats? Nah, just us then.
IGN UK Podcast #172: With Extra Lens Flare – 46.8 MB
(right click to save)

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #170: Do Not Be Alarmed
item.description:
AWOOGA! AWOOGA! Yes, that sound means it's time for this week's IGN UK Podcast, in which the team discusses everything from the sad demise of THQ and the divvying up of its corpse to the incredible Nintendo news blitz. We also address the flood of PS4 rumours we've heard, and then panic when the fire alarm goes off midway through recording.
Luckily Alex, Krupa, Chris and Stu weren't burnt to a crisp and made it through to the end of the podcast. But there's a lot to get through, so let's go!
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #163: Wii U or Won't You?
item.description:
The next generation of consoles is officially here. The Wii U came out this week in Europe and we've been playing with it all week.
We also talk about all the latest X-Men news and the revelation that Heath Ledger was almost Batman, not Joker.
If you haven't already seen them, here's our three part video series telling you everything you need to know about Nintendo's new console.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #162: Somethings You Might Have Missed
item.description:
Another week, another podcast.
This week we talk all about the two possible SKUs for the next-gen Xbox, Sony's junk, and the return of Lawrence Kasdan to the Stars Wars franchise. He wrote Empire Strikes Back! Believe, people.
We also discuss and run through some of the year's best games that you might have overlooked or never got around to playing.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices
item.content:encoded:
Another week, another podcast.

This week we talk all about the two possible SKUs for the next-gen Xbox, Sony's junk, and the return of Lawrence Kasdan to the Stars Wars franchise. He wrote Empire Strikes Back! Believe, people.

We also discuss and run through some of the year's best games that you might have overlooked or never got around to playing.

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #161: GTA V Full Disclosure
item.description:
Welcome to the IGN UK Podcast. Alex has seen Grand Theft Auto V at Rockstar HQ in New York City, and he can finally talk about it. He tells us everything he saw and we dissect the new trailer.
We get around to chatting about Tom Hardy being cast as Sam Fisher in the new Splinter Cell movie and the future of TimeSplitters.

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #158: Tom Braider
item.description:
Welcome fellow travellers of the internet highway. You've reached your destination: the IGN UK Podcast. Now, once you get over that feeling of disappointment, you'll be pleased to find out that on this show we talk about the following:
Warfighter, and what it's like to be shot in the throat by a BB gun
The continuing trials and tribulations of FIFA 13 on other platforms
The Tomb Raider box art
That new Iron Man 3 trailer
And the innovation (or bare-faced cheek) of Apple's new product line
So join Daniel, Stu, Tom, and Rich for this week's pod.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

Welcome fellow travellers of the internet highway. You've reached your destination: the IGN UK Podcast. Now, once you get over that feeling of disappointment, you'll be pleased to find out that on this show we talk about the following:

  • Warfighter, and what it's like to be shot in the throat by a BB gun
  • The continuing trials and tribulations of FIFA 13 on other platforms
  • The Tomb Raider box art
  • That new Iron Man 3 trailer
  • And the innovation (or bare-faced cheek) of Apple's new product line

So join Daniel, Stu, Tom, and Rich for this week's pod.

item.title: IGN UK Podcast #157: GMA-zing
item.description:
This week a hungover Luke, Keza, Chris and Rich recall the events of last night's Games Media Awards, where our very own Ms. MacDonald won the inaugural Games Writer of the Year award.
We also get round to hazily discussing the biggest news stories of the week as well as ponder just what the hell that mystery trailer of Sony's could be teasing.
Remember, if you've got something to say to the IGN UK team, grab us on Twitter page, our Facebook page, or via email at ignukfeedback@ign.com.
Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices
